bonuses.leadin: In this play, Risa, a follower of Prophet Samuel, slashes her legs with a razor to avoid being treated as a sex object. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this play that ends with Sterling smashing a butcher’s window to steal ham for his deceased friend, Hambone. Sterling, Wolf, and Holloway are among the patrons of Memphis Lee’s diner in this play.

In this play by the author of Two Trains Running, Alberta dies giving birth to Raynell, the daughter of former baseball player Troy Maxson. It ends with Troy’s brother, Gabriel, blowing a stopped trumpet to send Troy to heaven.
Two Trains Running and Fences are part of this African-American playwright’s ten-part Pittsburgh cycle.
answers_sanitized: August Wilson [or Frederick August Kittel, Jr.]
